texto: What she'd done was give him a glimpse of something that scared the bejesus out of him, something never meant for men like him that could start a hunger that would eat away what little was left inside him that didn't need to be shoved into the dark place.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ellen O'Connell: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “What she'd done was give him a glimpse of something that scared the bejesus out of him, something…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, thought-provoking, something pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e reflexao.

texto: Martha said, "Do you have any idea of the kind of surprise your brothers are in for sooner or later? Or are you doing it on purpose?" Cord put his hat on and pulled it low, hiding his eyes. "Grown man walks around with his eyes shut tight, he shouldn't be surprised if he bumps into something he didn't see. You aren't trying to convince anybody of anything they don't want to believe." Martha laughed. "You win. I just hope I'm there when the blind men hit the wall.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Ellen O'Connell que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Martha said, "Do you have any idea of the kind of surprise your brothers are in for sooner or lat…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, ignorance, martha, com foco em reflexao.

frase_original: Martha said, "Do you have any idea of the kind of surprise your brothers are in for sooner or later? Or are you doing it on purpose?" Cord put his hat on and pulled it low, hiding his eyes. "Grown man walks around with his eyes shut tight, he shouldn't be surprised if he bumps into something he didn't see. You aren't trying to convince anybody of anything they don't want to believe." Martha laughed. "You win. I just hope I'm there when the blind men hit the wall.

texto: Men get drunk and boast, or they get religion and repent.
explicacao: Citação de Ellen O' Connell sobre temas ligados a fe. “Men get drunk and boast, or they get religion and repent.” articula fe, boast, men em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como men e religion.
